ety: rt. Zend gared, fr. gar = S. गर् (गॄ)
def: s.m. Noise, clamour, exclamation, shout, uproar, tumult; a mob; — lamentation, complaint, groaning; growling; gurgling noise in the throat.
more: (or prop. g̠ẖireʼo ) s. m. 1. Noise, exclamation, shout, tumult. 2. A mob. 3. A guggling noise in the throat, growling.

subs: or garg-purohit, or garg-r̤ishi, or garg-muni, s.m. = garg, q.v.
vulg. garg-bansī, s.m. Name of a class of Rājpūts.

def: s.f. = gauraiyā, q.v.
more: n. f. S. गृहकु लाय. . A sparrow ( chiṛyā , 1).

def: s.m. A scaffold; — a castle, a tower; a bastion; — a cavalier.
more: n. m. H. gaṛh fort, gaj elephant. A bastion.
s. m. 1. A scaffold. 2. A tower, a bastion, a cavalier.

def: s.m. = گرگج gargaj, q.v. s.m. A scaffold; — a castle, a tower; a bastion; — a cavalier.
adj. (dialec.) Thick, dense, crowded, full, densely packed; miscellaneous, motley.
s.f. (Dakh.) Overflowing copiousness or plenty; great press or thronging; — a medley, mash; miscellany.
s.m. A whirlpool, an eddy; — a churn.
more: n. f. The rumbling sound made by the water in a hukka. gaṛ-gaṛ'ā , n. m. A large smoking pipe.

def: s.f. Gurgling, rumbling; the gurgling sound made by the water in a ḥuqqa.

ety: for A. غرغرة, inf. n. of غرغر 'to gargle'
def: s.m. Gargling; making a bubbling noise (as water in boiling, &c.); rattling sound in the throat; the death-rattle.
s.m. A common kind of ḥuqqa; a large smoking-pipe.
more: s. m. 1. Bustle, confusion. 2. Motion of the bowels, borborygmi.

def: s.f. = gaṛgaṛ, q.v. s.m. A whirlpool, an eddy; — a churn.
n. f. A rumbling sound.

ety: gargarā˚ = S. गर्गर+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment
def: v.n. To gurgle; to rumble; to roll, to thunder; to roar (as a tiger, &c.).
more: E., v. n. To rumble (the bowels, a hukka ).
v. a. To beg for in a whine.
v. n. 1. To gargle. 2. To thunder. 3. To roll. 4. To roar (as, a tiger), to rumble.

def: v.n. = gargarānā, q.v. v.n. To gurgle; to rumble; to roll, to thunder; to roar (as a tiger, &c.).
more: v. n. To thunder. ¶ guṛguṛānā, v. a. To rumble (the bowels). ¶ giṛgiṛānā, v. a. To beseech, to implore earnestly and humbly.

def: s.f. = gaṛgaṛāhaṭ = gaṛgaṛ, q.v. s.m. A whirlpool, an eddy; — a churn.
s.f. A gugglet, a water-vessel.
s.m. A kind of grass which grows in low grounds during the rains.
n. m. Rags; tatters.
s.m. Old tattered clothes, tatters, rags.
more: s. m. Any old tattered clothes, rags and tatters. See گودڙ gūdaṛ.

def: stronghold, defenses; citadel, fortress; castle, bastion
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀠 (gaḍha), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *gʰr̥dʰás, from Proto-Indo-European *gʰerdʰ-.

def: s.f. A difficulty; a misfortune.
s.m. A fort; citadel; castle; — a wheeling hovel, or engine of war, under cover of which soldiers approach a fort
more: (see گڐهہ) s. m. A castle, a fort.
subs: To take a fort; (fig.) to surmount a difficulty
s.f. Moat or trench of a fort; an entrenchment.

def: n. m. See kargah .

adj. (f. -ī), Thick (as cloth, &c., or a fluid mixture of any kind), close, dense, gross; muddy, turbid; close, intimate (friendship); deep (sleep, colour, &c.); stout, substantial; strong (infusion, &c.); stiff, difficult, arduous; sturdy, compact; bold, undaunted, valiant (in fight); sly, shrewd, knowing, wise, proficient, adept, smart; — s.m. A kind of coarse, thick cloth.
more: adj. 1. Thick, close, dense. 2. Sly, shrewd, knowing, wise. 3. Strong (as, tea).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀸𑀠 (gāḍha), from Sanskrit गाढ (gāḍha), from the root गाह् (gāh). Cognate with Nepali गाढा (gāḍhā), Sindhi ڳاڙهو (g̠āṛho) / ॻाढ़ो, Gujarati ગાઢું (gāḍhũ), Marathi गाढ (gāḍh), Kholosi gahro.

def: s.f. Censure, blame, reproach, abuse.
more: n. m. Dim. gaṛhaiyā , n. f. S. गर्त्त A pit; ravine. gaṛhā āṅṭnā, yā bharnā , v. a. 1. To fill a pit or hollow. 2. To make up a deficiency. 3. To take one's fill. dāṅt kā gaṛhā, yā /?/khānā , ( Med .) A hollow tooth; alveolus. gāṛhe baiṭhnā, Ṭhags , v. n. To be concealed; to lie in ambush.
n. m. A beam of a ship; the cross timber of a boat.

def: s.m. A hole, hollow, cavity, pit; a grave; a ravine; an abyss
more: s. m. A cavity, a hole, a pit, an abyss: (in dakh.) a valley (?).
subs: or gaṛhā bharnā, To fill up a hole or hollow; — to make up a deficiency; — to take (one's) fill
To lie concealed in a pit or hole, to lie in ambush.

def: adj. S. गाढ़ 1. Thick; close; dense. 2. Close; intimate. Gāṛhī dostī . Close friendship. 3. Difficult; burdensome; dire. Debī! apnī dayā kar, jīn pe gāḍhī bhīṛ! Play. O Goddess! pity show to them who suffer dire calamity! 4. Strong; good for fight. Yeh lāl baṛā gāṛhā hai .
more: n. m. Coarse Indian cloth. gaṛhvār', gaṛhvāṛ , adj. Coarse thick (cloth).

def: s.f. Burying.

ety: gaṛhā, q.v.+ī or iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A pit, a large hole; a small pond or tank.
s.f. Making, make, workmanship; malleating (jewellery); — the price of malleating jewellery, or of graving, &c.
s.m. A burier.
s.m. Censuring, blaming; censure; blame, reproach.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit ग्रहण (grahaṇa).

ety: caus. of gaṛhnā; — gaṛhā˚ = gaṛhāw˚ = Prk. गढ़ावे = घड़ावे = S. घट+आपय˚; rt. घट्
def: v.t. To cause to make, form, or fashion (especially by beating or hammering); to form, fashion; to malleate; to grave.
more: (caus. of گڙهنا) v. a. To cause to form by hammering, to cause to make.

def: v.t. To bury, &c.

ety: gāṛhā, q.v. + pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Thickness, closeness, denseness, &c. (see gāṛhā).
= S گارهستهيه गार्हस्थ्य gārhasthya (fr. gr̤iha - stha), Of or relating to the condition of a householder; fit for, or incumbent on, a householder.
= H گارهستهك गार्हस्थिक gārhasthik (fr. gr̤iha - stha), Of or relating to the condition of a householder; fit for, or incumbent on, a householder.

ety: gaṛh(nā) + at = Prk. अत्ती = S. आप्ति (i.e. āpi, caus. augment + aff. ti)
def: s.f. Make, form, fashion, shape, mould, build; workmanship ( = gha ṛat).
more: (from گڙهنا) s. f. Form, make, fashion.

def: s.f. A dialec. form of gaṛhat, q.v.
v.t. = gaṛhānā.
s.m. A hole, pit, cavity; a ditch; an abyss.
s.f. A small fort; a castle; a village fortification of mud flanked with towers (under former governments there was scarcely a village in India without its gaṛhī)
more: s. m. A small fort, a castle.
subs: s.m. A description of muʻāfī tenure, in Bundelkhand, by which lands are held on paying a stipulated yearly tribute (but not one-fifth the amount which ought to be paid)
To take a fort, &c. ( = gaṛh jītnā, q.v.).

def: s.m. = gaṛhelā, q.v. s.m. A hole, pit, cavity; a ditch; an abyss.

ety: gaṛh˚, rt. of gaṛhnā = gaṛnā+iyā = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इका
def: s.f. A pike, a short javelin, a small spear; a goad.

ety: gaṛhā, q.v.+ī or iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A pit, a large hole; a small pond or tank.
more: s. f. A pike, a javelin, spear. ¶ gaṛhaiyā, 1. s. f. (from S̱. گڙهنا) A small tank or pond. 2. s. m. A burier.

def: s.m. Name of a town and celebrated place of Hindū pilgrimage situated on the Ganges to the east of Meraṭ (a fair is held there in the month of Māgh).
v.t. (dialec.) = gāṛnā, q.v. v.t. To strain, filter; — to squeeze, press; to milk.
more: v. a. (dakh.) To bury: see گاڙنا gāṛnā.

ety: gaṛh˚ = Prk. गढ(इ) = S. घट(ते), rt. घट्
def: v.t. To make, form, fashion, shape; to forge; to form by hammering, to malleate; — to mould, cast; — to practise the goldsmith's or the founder's art (cf. ghaṛnā); — to smooth, plane, rasp; — v.n. To be made or fashioned (esp. by beating or hammering), to be formed; to be graved; to be moulded; to be cast, &c.
v.t. To malleate, to form by hammering, &c.
v.n. = gaṛnā, q.v. v.t. To strain, filter; — to squeeze, press; to milk.
more: 1. v. a. To malleate, form by hammering. 2. To make, form, shape, fashion. 3. v. n. To be made, formed, or fashioned.
v. n. To be driven down (as, a stake), to be buried, sunk, &c. See گڙنا gaṛnā.

r: gaṛhwār, gaṛhwāṛ, gaṛh, gāṛha, wār, wālā

def: adj. Coarse, thick (cloth, &c.); gross, fat.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Deserving reproach, contemptible, mean, low, vile; — a contemptible or mean man.
s.f. 1˚ = gālī, q.v.; — 2˚ corr. of gāṛī, q.v. adj. High, excessive, exorbitant; high-priced, dear; precious.
more: n. f. S. गन्त्री 1. A wheeled conveyance; a coach; carriage; car; cart; hackery. Kabhī nāo meṅ gāṛī, kabhī gāṛī meṅ nāo . Prov. Now see a cart upon the boat and now in cart a boat. (The whirligig of time). 2. A train; railway train. Pūrab kī gāṛī. gāṛī bhar . A cart-load. gāṛī bhar rastā , n. m. A street wide enough for a cart. gāṛī begār meṅ pakaṛnā . Forcible seizure of carts, etc. gāṛī jotnā , 1. To yoke bullocks or horses to a conveyance. 2. To let carts, etc. on hire. gāṛī chalānā , v. a. 1. To drive a cart. 2. To let or hire out carriages. gāṛī chhūṭnā , v. n. To start (a train). gāṛī-khānah , n. m. 1. A carriage house. 2. A cab-stand. gāṛī kā rāstah , n. m. A cart road. gāṛī kaṭnā , v. n. 1. To be robbed (a cart). 2. To be cut off (a carriage of a train). gāṛī hāṅknā , v. a. To drive a cart, etc. chaltī gāṛī meṅ roṛā aṭkānā , v. a. 1. To put a stone in the way of a running cart. 2…
s. f. Abuse. See گالي gālī.
in compos. s. f. Doing, making: see P. گري gar-ī.

def: s.f. A cart; car, carriage, coach; (for rel-gāṛī) railway-carriage, railway-train; — gāṛī-bhar, adj. & s.m. A cart-load of; a cart-load; — wide enough for a cart (to pass; e.g. gāṛī-bhar rastā)
more: s. f. A cart, car, coach, carriage. گاڙي وان gāṛī-wān, or گاڙي بان gāṛī-bān ( गंत्रीवाह ) s. m. A carter, carman, coachman.
subs: To harness a horse or horses (or to yoke bullocks) to a conveyance; — to let carts, &c. on hire
To drive a cart, &c.; — to hire out carriages
s.m. A carriage-house, coach-house; — a cab-stand
A cart to be robbed; — carriage (of a train) to be cut off
or gāṛī-wān, s.m. A carter, carman; carriage-driver, coachman

def: s.m. (dialec.) A friend, an intimate companion.

s.f. Courtesy or attention to strangers, hospitality; kindness to the poor ( = g̠arīb-parwarī).

ety: v.n. fr. غرب 'to become distant, to go far away,' &c.; see g̠urbat
def: adj. & s.m. Foreign, alien; strange, wonderful; rare, unusual, extraordinary; — poor, destitute; meek, mild, humble, lowly; — a stranger, foreigner, an alien; — a poor man; a meek or humble person
more: adj. pl. g/?/urabā . 1. Poor; destitute. 1. Ek garīb ko mārā thā to nau man charbī niklī thī . Prov. A poor man I once killed with nine maunds fat rewarded me. (The reply made to one who pleaded that the slaughter of so poor a man would be no gain to his destroyer). 2. Garīb ne rozah rakkhā, din baṛe huë . Prov. When poor men fast the day just lengthens out. (They cannot afford to feast well before and after the day's fast). 3. Garīb! tere tīn nām—jhūṭā, pājī, be-īmān! Poor man! thy names are three—liar, wretch, and rogue. 4. Garīb ko kauṛī asharfī . Prov. To a poor man a farthing is a sovereign. 2. Humble; meek; mild; innocent. Nainā baṛe garīb haiṅ, raheṅ palak kī oṭ, Ghāt paṛe chūke nahīṅ, kareṅ lākh meṅ choṭ . The eye so harmless seems retired behind its lid, Chance found it spareth not, a hundred thousand bleed! garīb-ul-vatan ; H. pardesī , A foreigner; stranger. garīb-harāmzādah ,…

(from غربت) 1. s. m. and f. A foreigner, a stranger. 2. adj. Poor, humble, mild, meek, strange, foreign, wonderful. (Pers. plur. غريبان The poor, &c.). غريب الوطن g̠ẖarību-l-wat̤an, In a foreign land, stranger. غريب پرور g̠ẖarīb-parwar, Cherisher of the poor. غريب پروري g̠ẖarīb-parwar-ī, s. f. Cherishing of the poor. غريب مار g̠ẖarīb-mār, s. f. Oppression of the poor. غريب نواز g̠ẖarīb-nawāz, adj. Courteous to strangers, kind to the poor, hospitable. غريب نوازي g̠ẖarīb-nawāz-ī, s. f. Hospitality, courtesy or attention to strangers, kindness to the poor.
subs: adj. & s.m. In a foreign land; — a foreigner, stranger
s.f. Cherishing of, or kindness to, the poor
s.m. The humble dwelling (of your servant), my house
or g̠arīb-kī mār, s.f. Oppression of the poor; misfortune
adj. & s.m. Courteous to strangers; kind to the poor, hospitable; — one who is kind to the poor ( = g̠arīb-parwar)
s.f. Courtesy or attention to strangers, hospitality; kindness to the poor ( = g̠arīb-parwarī).
ex: وہ ایک غَرِیب لَکَڑْہارے سے لَکْڑی کَٹْوانے گَیا ہَے۔
vo ek ġarīb lakaṛhāre se lakṛī kaṭvāne gayā hai.
He went to get the wood cut by a poor carpenter.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَرِیب (ġarīb), borrowed from Arabic غَرِيب (ḡarīb), from غَرُبَ (ḡaruba).

ety: for A. غريبة, fem. of g̠arīb
def: adj. & s.f. = g̠arīb, q.v.

ety: g̠arīb, q.v. + Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Fit for the poor; humble; — like a stranger; like a poor person; humbly.
more: (comp. of غريب and Pers. affix آنه) 1. adj. Fit for the poor, humble. 2. adv. Like a stranger or poor person.

ety: g̠arīb, q.v.+ī, Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Foreignness, strangeness; — poverty, indigence, wretchedness; meekness, mildness, lowliness, humility.
more: n. f. Poverty; penury; indigence; want. 2. Meekness; mildness ( ādhīnī , 2). garībī ānā , v. n. To be reduced to poverty.
s. f. 1. The state of being a foreigner, foreignness. 2. Humility, wretchedness.

def: vocative plural of غَرِیب (ġarīb)
oblique plural of غَرِیب (ġarīb)
adj. & s.m. Drowning; drowned; immersed, submerged; overwhelmed; — a drowning person
more: n. m. A drowning person. garīq , adj. Immersed; drowned. garīq-i-rahmat , adj. Overwhelmed with mercy; drowned in the grace of God. Khudā use garīq-i-rahmat kare! God bless him!

(verbal adj. from غرق) 1. s. m. A drowning person. 2. adj. Immersed, drowned. غريق آب g̠ẖarīḳ-āb, adj. Deep, out of manʼs depth. غريق رحمت g̠ẖarīḳ-i-raḥmat, adj. Overwhelmed with mercy, drowned in the grace of God.
subs: adj. Overwhelmed with mercy, drowned in the grace of God.

def: s.m. Agaric.
s.f. Heaviness, weight, gravity; importance, dignity, venerableness; worth, excellence; self-importance, vanity; boast.
wety: From Classical Persian گَرما (garmā, “heat”).

def: s.m. A forest, wood, thicket; covert, haunt, place of resort (of the lion, tiger, &c.).
more: s. A wood, a forest or thicket frequented by lions.

adj. Heaviest; excessively heavy; very venerable; worst.
s.m. A low hill, a hillock; a high mound or bank; an acclivity; a ravine; a steep or difficult pass.
more: s. m. A low hill or high mound, an acclivity, a ravine, steep or difficult pass.

s.m. = galiyā, q.v. (and cf. galiyar). s.m. A bullock that lies down in the midst of his work; — a lazy ox.
adj. Perverse, stubborn, obstinate, stiff-necked; stout-hearted.
s.f. Stoutheartedness; — stubbornness, obstinacy.

def: s.m. Meekness, gentleness, mildness, clemency, forbearance.

s.f.(?) (dialec.) = garjāṭ, and garjan, qq.v. s.f. (dialec.) Thundering, thunder; sound of thunder, &c.

ety: v.n. fr. غرق 'to drown, to sink'
def: s.m. Drowning, sinking, immersion; — adj. Drowned, immersed, sunk, overwhelmed; absorbed, engrossed, deep (in)
more: adj. 1. Drowned; immersed; sunk. Nashe meṅ garq paṛā hai . 2. Absorbed; given up to; occupied with. garq k .; Illit. gaṛap k. , v. a. Rare. To drown. garq h .; Illit. gaṛap h. , v. n. See دَوبنا 1, 3.
1. s. m. Drowning, immersion, sinking. 2. adj. Drowned, immersed, sunk. غرق هونا g̠ẖarḳ honā, To be immersed, to be absorbed (in meditation, &c.), to drown.

(-meṅ), To drown; to be immersed (m), be sunk or steeped (in); to be deep (in), be absorbed (by), be engrossed (in).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَرْق (ġarq), from Arabic غَرْق (ḡarq).

def: adj. Submerged, overflowed, inundated; — s.f. Flooding land; flood, inundation; flooded land; low ground
more: n. f. 1. Flood; inundation. 2. Depression; low ground. 3. See bishṭī. garqī meṅ ānā , v. n. 1. To be flooded. 2. To be lowered or depressed (ground). Dālān garqī meṅ ā gayā .
subs: To be flooded; to lie low; to be lowered or depressed (ground).

def: adj. Light-blue, grey (in colour, like the eyes of a cat).
s.m. (dialec.) = gaḍḍā, q.v. s.m. A soft quilted bedding; a large cushion (cf. gaddī); — a sheaf or bundle of fodder (ćarī).
s.m. Rock or fossil salt (especially that found in the district of Sānbhar, in the province of Ajmīr).
hothouse, greenhouse, glasshouse
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَرْمْخَانَه (garmxāna, “greenhouse”), by analysis گَرْم (garm, “hot”) + خانَہ (xāna, “place”).

ety: Pehl. garm; Zend garema; S. घर्म
def: adj. Hot, warm; in a state of heat; burning; glowing: fervid; ardent, zealous, fervent; excited; eager, intent on; fiery, choleric, virulent; active, lively, brisk (as a market, &c.)
more: adj. 1. Warm (تتا). 1. Harī harī ḍāl-vāle tāze aur garm kā mazā! (Bhuṭṭā ). Delhī Street cry. 2. Din ko sharam, rāt ko bagal garam . Prov. By day such shame she shows, at night her bosom glows. 2. Burning; glowing; fervent; eager ( sargarm ). 3. Choleric ( tez , 6). 4. In heat; excited. 5. Lively; brisk. Garm bāzār. garm ikhtilātī, garm-sohbat , n. f. Warmth of friendship; strong attachment. garm-bāzārī , n. f. Brisk demand; good sale. garm bolnā , v. a. To speak angrily. garm-joshī , n. f. Warmth of affection; zeal; enthusiasm. garm khabar . The latest news; the current rumour. garm-sard , adj. 1. Hot and cold. 2. Lukewarm. 3. The ups and downs of life. garm sard uṭhānā, yā sahnā , v. n. To endure sufferings. garm kapṛe , n. m. Warm or winter clothes. garm k. , 1. To warm; heat. 2. To incense; put in a passion. 3. To spur; incite. garm mizāj . A hot temper. garm h. , v. n. 1. To become …

adj. 1. Hot, warm. 2. Ardent, active, zealous, eager, intent on, fiery, cholerick, virulent. 3. Thronged or crowded (a market or assembly). گرم اختلاطي garm-iḵẖtilāt̤-ī, s. f. Warmth of friendship. گرم بازاري garm-bāzār-ī, s. f. Throng in a market, great demand, high value or estimation (of any thing). گرم بولنا garm-bolnā, To speak readily and to the purpose. گرم جوشي garm-josh-ī, Warmth, affection, cordiality, ebullition. گرم رو garm-rau, adj. Going hastily, a lover travelling in haste to his beloved. گرم ره garm-rah, Travelling or going hastily. گرم سخن garm-suḵẖan, Speaking with warmth or forcibly. گرم شتاب garm-shitāb, adj. Hastening. گرم نظاره garm-naz̤āra, adj. Looking eagerly. گرم هونا garm-honā, To be angry, to become hot, to be thronged (a market).
adj. Fervent, fervid. گرم کرنا garm karnā, To warm.
subs: adj. Hot and hot, piping hot, all hot; — new, fresh; — spontaneous, unpremeditated, extempore
and garm-ṣǒḥbat, s.f. Warmth of friendship, strong attachment, close companionship, intimacy
s.f. Activity or briskness in a market, brisk (or great) demand, good sale; high value or estimation (of anything)
To speak warmly or angrily; to speak readily and to the purpose
s.f. Warmth of affection, cordiality; ebullition; empressement, ardour, zeal, enthusiasm
adj. & s.m. Going hastily, pressing on hotly; — a lover travelling in haste to his beloved
adj. Speaking with warmth; speaking forcibly or effectively
or garm-o-sard, adj. & s.m. Hot and cold; lukewarm; — ups and downs (of life); prosperity and adversity; accidents or vicissitudes (of time or fortune)
adj. & adv. Hastening; in hot haste
v.t. To warm, heat; to make angry, to excite, incense; to make eager or active, to stimulate, spur, incite; to exercise
vulg. garam masālā, s.m. Spices, seasoning
v.n. To be or become hot; to become heated or excited; to be or become angry; to be busy, or active, or brisk, to be thronged (a market, &c.); to be rife.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَرْم (garm).

def: s.m. (uninflec.) Heat, warmth, sultriness; the hot season, summer
more: s. m. (uninflect.) Summer. گرماگرم garm-ā-garm, Hot and hot.
wety: From Classical Persian گَرما (garmā, “heat”).

def: s.m. A hot (or tepid) bath.

adj. 1. Hot and hot; piping hot; smoking. 2. New; fresh. 3. Spontaneous; extempore. Kuchh rukhāī thī, kuchh haṅsī, kuchh sharm, Tāze fiqre, latīfe garmā-g̣arm . Wom. Shauq. A little dry, a budding smile, and something shy, Bon mots fresh from her lips anew flashed constantly. garmā garmī se , adv. Zealously; ardently.

ety: garmā(nā) + āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Becoming heated or hot; heat, warmth (cf. garmāʼī).
s.f. Becoming heated or hot; heat; excitement; — a stimulant
more: n. f. A stimulant. garmāī ānā , 1. To be hot (a thing). 2. To be excited (with lust).
subs: (-ko), To become heated; to be hot (a thing); to be or become excited or inflamed (with passion or lust).

ety: garm, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To become warm or heated or hot ( = garm honā); to be or become angry; to become enlivened
more: v. n. To be hot, angry, etc. See garm . 1. Jitnā garmāëgā utnā hī ḅarsegā . Prov. The hotter it grows the more rain it bestows. 2. Sakhī deve aur sharmāë, bādal barse aur garmāë . Prov. The liberal gives, his cheeks with shame suffuse; The cloud pours rain and heat renews. 3. Tum to zarā sī bāt meṅ garmā jāte ho!
v. n. 1. To be angry. 2. To become enlivened.

ety: garm, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Heat, warmth; warm or hot weather, the hot season; warmth, glow; fervour, fervency, ardour; activity, briskness, throng (of a market); — heat of temperament; heat of system, morbid heat; fieriness, vehemence; passion, rage, anger, excitement; attachment, warm affection; sexual passion, lust; heat; — the venereal disease
more: n. f. 1. Heat; warmth. 2. The hot weather; summer. 3. Warmth; glow; fervour ( sargarmī ). Sīnah phaṛak rahā hai khūbāṅ ke dard o gam meṅ, Paṭṭhoṅ meṅ woh kahāṅ haiṅ jo garmiyāṅ haiṅ ham meṅ . The old man boastful. Nazīr. My breast beats high with passion for the fair, Where is the youth can show a warmth so rare ? 4. Passion; rage; anger; excitement. 5. Attachment; love. 6. Sexual passion; heat; furor . 7. The venereal disease ( ātshak ). garmī paṛnā , v. n. To be hot. garmī-dānah ; Sant. bal-bal bumbrī , n. f. Prickly heat. garmī kā zor , n. m. 1. Excess of heat. 2. A discharge of blood with the urine (elephants, horses). garmī kā ilm ( Phys .) Heat. garmī k. , v. a. 1. To produce warmth. 2. To be angry with. 3. To make a show of affection. garmī kī had , ( Geog .) The tropics. garmī ke kapṛe , Summer clothes. garmī h. , v. n. 1. To suffer from heat. Bachche ko garmī ho gaī hai! 2. To h…
s. f. 1. Warmth, ardour, heat, fieriness; throng (of a market). 2. The venereal disease. گرمي کرنا garm-ī karnā, To affect fondness.
subs: v.n. To be hot (the weather; e.g. aj baṛī garmī paṛtī hai, 'it is very hot to-day')
Excess or predominance of heat (of the weather, or of the temperament or system); a discharge of bloody urine (a disease in elephants, horses, &c.)
(-meṅ), To produce heat or warmth (in); to become angry (with, -se); to make a show of affection, to affect fondness (for)
s.m. pl. Clothes for the warm weather, summer clothing
(apnī), To satisfy sexual passion or lust; to indulge in carnal intercourse
(-ko), To suffer from heat of the system; — to have the venereal disease
s.f. Cold and heat; luke-warmness (in affection)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَرْمِی (garmī), from Middle Persian 𐭢𐭥𐭬𐭩𐭧 (gʿmyḥ /⁠garmīh⁠/), from Proto-Iranian *garmáh, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *gʰarmás, from Proto-Indo-European *gʷʰer- + *-mós. First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi گرمی (grmy /⁠garmī⁠/). By surface analysis, گَرْم (garm) + ی (ī).

def: to get hot
direct plural of گَرْمی (garmī)
oblique plural of گَرْمی (garmī)
vocative plural of گَرْمی (garmī)

ety: gār˚ = S. गालय(ति), caus. of rt. गल्
def: v.t. To strain, filter; — to squeeze, press; to milk.
more: v. a. S. गारण To squeeze; press; extract juice.

v. a. S. गर्त्त a hole. 1. To plant; pitch; fix; sink. 2. To drive in; hammer (a nail). 3. To bury; inter; lay in the grave. 1. Gāṛ ke rakkho! Bury it! 2. Jīte ko gāṛ dūṅgā! 3. Mujh ko tū gāṛe, dugānā! apnā jī bhāri na kar! Le gayā jo lūṭ-kar ghar chor, mat ṭasve bahā! Wom. Rangīn. 4. Rāt ko gār dharī, subah ughāṛ lī . Hin. Wom. Buried at night, raked up in the morning. (A quarrel). 4. To cover with ashes (fire).
v. a. 1. To strain. 2. To squeeze. 3. To milk.

ety: gāṛ˚, prob. Prk. गाढ(इ), fr. S. गाढ, p.p.p. of rt. गाह्; cf. gāṛhnā and gaṛhnā
def: v.t. To drive (in or into), drive down, to sink, to imbed (in), to fix firmly; to unite; to bury; to cover (a fire) with ashes; to set, to plant
more: v. a. To bury, to set, to drive down, to fix, to sink.

def: v.n. A dialec. form of gaṭhnā, q.v.
more: v. n. S. गर्त्त See gāṛnā , v. a. To sink; to be depressed. 1. Āṅkheṅ gaṛnā . 2. Jahāṅ ke murde tahāṅ hī gaṛte haiṅ . Prov. Where'er one dies he buried lies. (As the tree falls so must it lie). gaṛā-huā khazānah , n. m. Buried treasure.

def: v.n. To penetrate (into, -meṅ), to enter (into), to stick (into), to pierce; to be driven (into), to be firmly fixed (in); to sink (in or into); to be put (into the ground), to be buried; — s.m. A mark, sign, trace
more: v. n. To penetrate, enter, be driven into the earth (as, a stake, &c.), be fixed, fix, lie, sink, be buried.

def: s. (dakh.) A mortar (small, in gunnery: Harris ).
Granada (a city and province of Andalusia, Spain)
wety: Borrowed from Arabic غَرْنَاطَة (ḡarnāṭa).

def: oblique plural of غار (ġār, “cave”)

ety: gaṛo˚ = gaṛāw˚ = gāṛ(nā)+Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.t. = gaṛānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to enter, cause to penetrate; to thrust (into), stick (into), drive (into), to poke or push (anything into); to pierce, prick; to put into a hole (in the ground), to bury
more: (causal of گڙنا) v. a. To pierce, to perforate, to bore, to stick into.

def: v. a. See chubhonā . To perforate; bore; stick into; drive in.
s.m. A (child's) go-cart (i.q. gaḍūlnā, q.v.). s.m. A child's go-cart ( = gaṛoṛnā).

ety: prob. S. गण्ड, or गडु+paṅkh, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Large-winged; — a large-winged bird; (met.) one who wears loose wide garments.
n. m. 1. A large winged bird. 2. ( Fac .) One who wears loose wide garments.
s.m. A deceiver.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَار (ġār), borrowed from Arabic غَار (ḡār). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi غار (ġar /⁠ġār⁠/).

ety: for A. غرة, fr. غرّ, inf. n. of غرّ 'to deceive'
def: adj. Deceived; haughty, proud; cross; — s.m. Pride, vanity ( = g̠urūr).
more: n. m. lit . a white spot on the forehead of a horse. 1. ( Pop. garrah ) An iron grey or roan horse. 2. The first day of the moon. 3. A fast. gurrā batānā, yā denā , v. a. To put off; to turn a deaf ear to; give the go-bye. 1. Āj khāne ko gurrah do! 2. Tīs din chāṅd ke vāde pe phirātā hai mujhe , A creditor. Chāṅd hotā hai to phir gurrah batātā hai mujhe . He puts me off for thirty days and says 'come next new moon,' When new moon comes again he puts me off with 'next new moon.'
n. m. Pride; vanity. Abhī iskā garrah nahīṅ ḍhayā . Mah. Wom.
1. adj. Haughty, proud. 2. Cross. 3. s. m. Pride.

s. m. 1. Whiteness, brightness. 2. A white spot on the forehead of a horse. 3. Morning or its splendour. 4. The first day of the moon, calends, the first and best of any thing, the forehead, front. 5. A fast, fasting.

def: adj. Reddish, copper-coloured (pigeon, &c.); bay-coloured, bay (horse); dark iron-grey (horse).
more: adj. Cor. of A. g/?/urrah . 1. Reddish (a pigeon); a coppery color. 2. Dark iron grey (a horse).
adj. Bayish (colour in a horse or pigeon).

ety: fem. of اغرّ ag̠arr, fr. g̠urra, q.v.
def: adj. White; bright, shining, splendid, conspicuous; fair, open, generous, noble, illustrious.
more: adj. Splendid, shining, conspicuous, noble, illustrious. ¶ g̠ẖurrā, s. m. See غره g̠ẖurra.

def: s.f. A pulley (esp. that over which the rope is passed in drawing water from a well); a reel (or other instrument) for twisting thread or string.
more: n. f. 1. A reel on which string is twisted. 2. A haystack; a rick. 3. A large stack of wheat or barley. 4. A small mound raised between heaps of corn and bhūsā on the threshing floor.
s. f. An instrument for twisting a thread or string.

ety: prob. = kharhī, q.v.; — or S. गड+इका
def: s.f. A haystack; a stack, rick (of wheat, barley, &c.); a small mound raised between heaps of corn and chaff on the threshing-floor.

ety: prob. fr. S. गर्हितः; or gar = gār = gālī+nindā, q.v.
def: adj. Abusive; scurrilous; ignominious, bad.
n. m. A snake-charmer.
s.m. A cavity, a hole; a ditch.

ety: rt. of gāṛ-nā + top, or thop˚, qq.v.
def: s.f. Burying, burial; — a tomb.
s.f. (dialec.) A decent and reputable woman (ʻone who is attached to home'), a modest or chaste woman.
s.m. (Dakh.) A friend.
more: s. m. (dakh.) Friend, companion: see يار yār.

ety: rt. of gāṛ-nā + top, or thop˚, qq.v.
def: s.f. Burying, burial; — a tomb.
adj. (f. -ī), Heavy, weighty; important; venerable, respectable; — demure.
more: n. m. S. गड्डक Dim. gaṛuī . 1. A water-pot. 2. A flower-pot carried about by musicians and dancing women at the feast of Basant as an offering to temples of saints or to people of rank from whom they receive presents in return.

def: s.m. A kind of water-pot; a narrow-mouthed vase or vessel with flowers in it (such a vase is carried about by musicians and dancing women at the feast of basant-panćamī as an offering to people of rank, from whom they receive presents).
more: s. m. 1. A kind of water-pot. 2. A flower-pot carried about by musicians and dancing women at the feast of basant-panchamī, as an offering to people of rank, from whom they receive presents in return.

ety: garuʼā, q.v. + āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता = इका
def: s.f. = garwāpan, q.v. s.m. Heaviness, weightiness; weight, load; gravity, importance; venerableness; respectability; — demureness.

s.m. (in Myth.) Name of a mystical bird, the chief of the feathered race, the enemy of the serpent race, and the vehicle of Vishṇu; — a large kind of crane, the adjutant-bird, Ardea argala (syn. haṛ-gillā); — a large vulture; — the great horn-bill, Homraius bicornis; — name of a peculiar military array
subs: s.m. Name of the seventeenth Purāṇa (it is the one commonly read after the death of a Hindū); (fig.) an old story.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit गरुड (garuḍá).

def: adj. Of or relating to Garuṛ; — s.m. An emerald.

s.m. = garuḍ, q.v. s.m. (in Myth.) Name of a mystical bird, the chief of the feathered race, the enemy of the serpent race, and the vehicle of Vishṇu; — a large kind of crane, the adjutant-bird, Ardea argala (syn. haṛ-gillā); — a large vulture; — the great horn-bill, Homraius bicornis; — name of a peculiar military array
more: s. m. 1. The bird and vehicle of Vish̤ṇu (he is generally represented as a being something between a man and a bird, and considered as sovereign of the feathered race). 2. A large kind of heron (Ardea argala), gigantick crane or adjutant bird.

def: adj. & s.m. Neutralizing the effect of poison; — a charmer; a dealer in antidotes.
s.m. (dialec.) A snake-charmer; a dealer in antidotes; a juggler (i.q. gāraṛū); a mountebank, a buffoon (cf. gāruṛī).

s.m. Proudness, pride, vainness, vanity; arrogance
adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Proud, haughty, &c. ( = garvit, q.v.); — garv-vantī, s.f. A proud woman.

v.t. To cause to be driven (into the ground); to cause to be stuck (into), to cause to be sunk; to cause to be buried.

ety: for A. g̠ashsh, inf. n. of غشّ 'to act dishonestly or insincerely towards,' &c.
def: s.f. Adulteration; adulterating, alloy, coating of base metal (on silver or gold).
more: n. m. A faint; swoon; stupor. gash ānā , v. n. To faint; go off in a swoon; swoon. gash h. , v. n. 1. To fall in love; to love. Yeh bhī un par gash haiṅ . 2. To take a great fancy to; to be mad after.
(for غشي or غشية) s. m. Stupor, fainting. غش آنا g̠ẖash ānā or غش پڙنا g̠ẖash paṛnā, To faint.

ety: contrac. of A. غشي g̠ashyuṉ, inf. n. غشی 'to become senseless, to swoon'
def: s.m. A swoon, stupor, insensibility, fainting
subs: or g̠ash paṛnā (-par), To swoon, to become insensible, to faint
(-par), To be dazzled, or bewildered, or astonished (at the sight of a beautiful object, &c.); to lose (one's) heart (to), to be enamoured (of), to fall in love (with).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غش (ġaš), from Arabic غَشْي (ḡašy).

ety: v.n. fr. غشم 'to take a course at random,' &c.
def: adj. (One) who goes, or acts, at random or in a headlong manner; (one) whom nothing will turn from his purpose; intrepid; headstrong.
more: adj. One who carries his head erect, one whom nothing turns from his purpose.

def: s.f. Fainting, becoming insensible; a swoon ( = g̠ash).
more: 1. adj. Fainting. 2. s. f. Fainting, swooning.

def: s.m. The covering of a (horse's) saddle, a saddle-cloth (plur. g̠awāshī).
more: (from غشو) s. m. A saddlecloth, a covering for a saddle.

def: adj. & s.m. Knotted, knotty; knotted or tied together; — a knot (in cloth, or cotton, &c.); — the knot tied in the skirts of a bride and bridegroom (see gaṭh-bandhan).
more: s. m. 1. Knots in cloth. 2. See گٿهبندهن gaṭh-bandhan.

ety: gaṭhwā(nā) + āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. The cost or charge for mending (shoes).

v.t. To cause to be joined, to cause to be tied together, to get two (things) joined or tied together; to have (things) tied up in a bundle; — ( = gaṭhānā), to cause to join or adhere; to tie together; to mend or cobble (shoes).
more: (causal of گٿهنا) v. a. To cause to join or to adhere or to tie together.

def: Caus. of گانتَهنا Jinke tavele bīch—kaī din kā zikr hai— Hargiz irāqī o arabī kā na thā shumār, Ab dekhtā hūṅ maiṅ kī zamāne ke hāth se Mochī se ḳafsh-i-pā ko gaṭhāte haiṅ woh udhār . Saudā. Stables they had—I speak of days gone by— Where Turkish, Arab horses countless were, But now time's heavy hand I here descry, Cobblers on credit mend the shoes they wear. gaṭhvā'ī , n. f. The charge for mending (shoes).

ety: fr. gaṭṭhā, q.v.; — āṅsī = S. अंश+इका
def: s.f. The twentieth part of a gaṭṭhā (i.e. about five inches).
s. (dakh.) Gout ( Harris ).

ety: gāt, q.v. + ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A cloth or dress worn (by village people) like a plaid (a ćaddar passed over the shoulders, and under the arms, and fastened in a knot on the chest)
more: n. f. A piece of cloth worn round the shoulder as a Tartan plaid. Gātī to chhātī dhake, or ḍhake pāgṛī sīs, Biyāj napūtā ke ḍhake kare pāṅch ke bīs . Rus. The mantle hides the breasts, the turhan screens the head, The childless usurer's hands from five on twenty spread. gātī bāṅdhnā yā mārnā , v. a. To put on as a Tartan plaid.
subs: or gātī mārnā, To wear or fasten on a gātī, to wear a ćādar as a plaid is worn.

def: s.f. Going, moving; motion; movement; pace; gait; march; passage, progress; procession; path, way, course; flight; — diurnal motion (of a planet in its orbit); a whole revolution (of a heavenly body); — procedure, carriage, deportment; — coming, arriving at, obtaining, attainment, access, reach, range; — end, termination, issue, event; course of events; fate, fortune, destiny; — an expedient, means; a means of success; way, art, or method of acting; stratagem; — basis; refuge, resource, remedy; — state, condition, situation, appearance, predicament, plight (as that in which one is beaten soundly), pickle, trim, mess; — mode of existence, nature; — a happy issue; happiness; salvation; transmigration (of souls); — funeral rites; — a period of life (as youth, manhood, old age); — a musical time or measure; an air, a tune; a quaver (in music); — a mode of dancing
subs: An air to be played; — to be soundly thrashed
v.t. To reduce to a miserable plight, to beat to a mummy; — to set up an 'Aunt-Sally'
or gat nāćnā, To dance to music
adj. (f. -kī), Of good quality, good, fine (as cloth, &c.)
(-kī), To make use (of), to apply; — to perform the funeral rites (of); — to reduce to a fearful plight, to beat to a mummy, to pommel

v.n. To be made use of; to come into use; — to be reduced to a fine plight, to be thrashed or pommelled soundly; — funeral rites to be performed; to obtain salvation (or to escape transmigration), to attain to final happiness.

def: s.f. = gat, q.v. s.m. A troop, band, gang, throng, crowd, multitude
more: adj. (dakh.) Crafty, cunning, knowing, evasive ( Harris ), clever.
subs: ( = g̠aṭ-paṭ), s.m. Close connection, sticking together, cohesion, mixture, jumble, confusion; conflict, shock, encounter; congressus; — adj. Crowded, jumbled together, in a confused mass, higgledy-piggledy; interlocked in fight; in close embrace.

def: activity; working; behavior

ety: gati or gat, q.v. + Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: s.m. One who plays the drum, i.e. the ḍhol, or t̤abla, &c.
more: One who plays on the tablā , etc. ( tabalchī ).

def: s.f. = gaṭhiyā or gaṭṭhī, qq.v. s.f. A bundle, package, &c. ( = gaṭṭhī); a bag, sack; a pannier; — a bump (see gaṭṭā); — pain in the joints; rheumatism; gout (in the joints)
subs: or gaṭhiyā-bāʼo, s.f. Rheumatism; gout in the joints.

def: s.m. = gadkā, q.v. s.m. A mace, a club; a bat; a stick; a foil or blunt wooden sword (used in fencing).

adj. & s.m. = guṭṭā, q.v. s.m. (dialec.) Exclusive right (of sale, supply, &c.); monopoly, contract; — an income of variable amount sold (or let) for a fixed sum; lease (of land, &c.), farm (syn. ijāra).
s.f. & adv. See s.v. gaṭ. s.m. A troop, band, gang, throng, crowd, multitude
subs: ( = g̠aṭ-paṭ), s.m. Close connection, sticking together, cohesion, mixture, jumble, confusion; conflict, shock, encounter; congressus; — adj. Crowded, jumbled together, in a confused mass, higgledy-piggledy; interlocked in fight; in close embrace.

def: s. f. Higgledy-piggledy.

Slang, adv. 1. Mixed together promiscuously. 2. Interlocked in fight or coition. Raṅgīṅ se aur mujh se ho jāë jis meṅ gaṭ-paṭ, Batlā de koī aisī tadbīr, merī bājī! Rangīn. O might Rangin and I be brought together! Tell me, O sister, how to weave this tether? gaṭ paṭ h . (Slang) v. n. 1. To be mixed up with. 2. To have sexual intercourse with.
s.m. A limb; the body ( = gāt, q.v.). s.m. A troop, band, gang, throng, crowd, multitude
subs: ( = g̠aṭ-paṭ), s.m. Close connection, sticking together, cohesion, mixture, jumble, confusion; conflict, shock, encounter; congressus; — adj. Crowded, jumbled together, in a confused mass, higgledy-piggledy; interlocked in fight; in close embrace.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit गात्र (gā́tra), ultimately from the root गा (gā). Doublet of گَات (gāt).

def: s.m. = gaṭṭā, q.v. s.m. Pasteboard, &c.
s.m. Pasteboard, &c.
more: n. m. H. gāṅṭh . a knot. 1. ( ḍāṭ ) A cork; stopper; plug; the tompion of a gun. 2. Ankle bone; wrist bone. Hāth yā pāoṅ kā gaṭṭā . 3. The padding round the pipe where it enters the hukka bottom. 4. Coarse old mortar. 5. A sweetmeat. 6. A corn on the toe. gol-gaṭṭā , n. m. A game played with pebbles. Chal, sāthan, gol-gaṭṭe kheleṅ! Rus. gaṭṭe paṛnā , v. n. To have corns on the foot. māthe par gaṭṭā paṛnā , v. n. lit . to get bumps on the head. To pray frequently.

def: s.m. A hard lump; a callosity, a corn (on the foot or toe); — the wrist-joint; ankle-bone; pastern (of a horse); — a plug, stopper, cork; tompion (of a gun); — the padding round the part of the tube of a ḥuqqa which fixes into the mouth of the metal bottom; — a lump of coarse old mortar; — a sweetmeat
more: s. m. The part of the pipe which fixes into the top of the metal part of a ḥuḳḳa.
subs: (-par), Callosities or corns to form (on the feet, &c.); to get hard lumps or bumps (on the head, from frequent prostations in prayer)
s.m. A boys' game (played with pebbles).

def: s.m. A large bundle, a pack, package, bale; a load; — the wrist, wrist-bone; ankle-joint; pastern (of a horse; see gaṭṭā); — a root (of onion, or of turmeric, &c.), a clove (of garlic); — a knot or division (in a measuring line or chain); the twentieth part of a jarīb (each gaṭṭhā containing three ilāhī gaz, or ninety-nine inches.
more: s. m. A bundle, package, pack; root of onion, turmerick, &c. a clove of garlick.

def: n. m. 1. A package; load ( gāṅṭh , 6). 2. ( ganṭhī ) A clove of onion. 3. The 20th part of a jarīb , each gaṭhā containing three ilāhī gaz , q. v.
n. m. A large bundle.
adj. Knotted, knotty; compact, stout, robust; — s.m. A large bundle, a bale, pack, package.
more: s. m. A large bundle, a bale, a pack.

def: n. f. A small bundle of any thing.
s.f. A small bundle (of anything); a ball; a pack, package; bale (of cotton); a pad (for the back of a beast of burden).
s.f. (dialec.) Bank (of a river or tank).
s.f. (Dakkhinī) A lump (of earth or clay), a clod; hard clay; coarse old mortar (cf. gaṭṭā).
s.m. (rt. gam), Motion; progress; way, course (of life, &c.); — (rt. gai), a singer; a celestial chorister.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Jāṭs.
s.m. Name of a tribe of Jāṭs.
s.m. A ditch; a hollow, pit, hole, cavity; den (of a wild beast).
more: n. f. A cow. gaū , adj. 1. Simple. Gaū brāhman . 2. Meek; gentle; inoffensive.
s. (dakh.) A measure of four kos.

def: s.m. (Dakh.) A measure of four kos.

def: s.f. A sprout, a shoot; a bud.

ety: prob. Prk. गुंछिआ; S. गुच्छ + इका
def: s.f. A sprout, a shoot; a bud.
more: n. f. S. गुच्छू clump of grass. A sprout; shoot.

def: s.f. A hole; a pit (prob. orig. the foot-mark of a cow; cf. goshpad, s.v. go).
s.f. A hole; a pit (prob. orig. the foot-mark of a cow; cf. goshpad, s.v. go).
more: s. f. A hole or pit.

def: n. m. An offering of a cow. gaū-ghāṭ . The side of a tank, etc. at which cows drink.
s.m. Noise, clamour, uproar, hubbub, commotion, tumult, disturbance, brawl.
more: s. m. Noise, disturbance, uproar, clamour, alarm, brawl.

def: adj. & s.m. Noisy, clamorous, tumultuous, &c.; — false; senseless, nonsensical; — a noisy or turbulent fellow; a disturber, &c.
more: Wom. adj. 1. Noisy; clamorous; uproarious. 2. Nonsensical; unmeaning; trashy. 3. False; mendacious.

def: s.f. = gauṅh, q.v. s.f. Continued effort, perseverance, diligence; pertinacity.
s.m. A village made over to any person on a permanent settlement with all the privileges of zamīndār; — a village or villages, the country (syn. dihāt).
n. m. The country.
s.f. A village; the lands of a village (esp. those immediately around it), lands or fields on which cattle graze.
s.m. Nature, essence, substance, stuff, matter; form; origin, root, stock, extraction; seed, offspring; — any hidden virtue; — intellect; wisdom; — a pearl; jewel, gem, precious stone
more: s. m. 1. A pearl, a gem, a jewel. 2. Lustre of a gem or sword. 3. Essence, origin, nature, substance. گوهر افشان gauhar-afshān, adj. Scattering pearls. گوهربار gauhar-bār, adj. Raining gems. گوهرسنج gauhar-sanj, Weighing or examining pearls; a critick.
subs: (of a gem, or a sword; — cf. jauhar)
adj. & s.m. Weighing or examining pearls; — a weigher or examiner of pearls (lit. or fig.); a critic
adj. Scattering gems; — elegant (discourse); s.m. An elegant discourse or composition.

def: adj. Innate, essential; — generous, noble; of good stock, high-bred; — s.m. A dealer in precious stones; a jeweller; a lapidary.
s.m. A portico (syn. nautā); a threshold; a gallery (syn. ćhajjā).
n. m. 1. A threshold. 2. A portico.

n. f. Shoes made of cow's hide. gaū-lochan , n. m. A yellow substance obtained from cow's urine, or the concrete bile of the cow. gaū-mutr , n. m. Cow's urine. gaū-mukhī , n. f. A cloth bag containing a rosary, the hand being thrust in to count the beads. gaū-medh , n. f. The sacrifice of cows. gaū-hattyā ; Brij. khurtī , n. f. The sin of killing a cow.
s.m. (Dakh.) corr. of gwālī = gwālā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Cow-keeping; — a cow-keeper, cow-herd, &c. ( = gwāl).
more: s. m. (dakh.) Cow-keeper, milkman, goat-herd ( Harris ).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गोलिका (golikā). Cognate with Persian گلوله (golule).

def: s.f. (Dakh.), corr. of gwālin, q.v.
W. Hin. v. n. To feel; receive an impression. Yeh garmī sardī ko jarā nāhīṅ gaultā!

ety: prob. S. गमनं 'way, possibility'; rt. गम्
def: s.f. Opportunity, advantage, occasion; need
more: n. m. A shady place in a field where oxen are tied.
s. f. m. Opportunity, advantage. گون گير gaun-gīr, Controller, uncontrolled, ruler, invested with authority, self-interested.
subs: s.m. A self-interested, or a selfish person; a time-server (syn. ḵẖẉud-g̠araẓ)
or gauṅ-gīrā, adj. & s.m. Uncontrolled; invested with authority; — self-interested, selfish; designing; — a controller; a ruler; — a selfish or designing person; time-server ( = gauṅ-kā yār)
or gauṅ gāṅṭhnā (apnī), To attain (one's) end, serve (one's) purpose.

def: s.m. (Braj) = gawan = gaman, qq.v.; — a place of resort, a retreat; a shady place (in a field) where oxen are tied.
adj. Unessential; subordinate, secondary; inferior; — metaphorical, figurative.
s.m. Bringing home a wife (from her father's) to her husband's house when she arrives at the age of puberty.
more: n. m. S. गमन Bringing home the betrothed wife to her husband's from her father's house when she arrives at the age of puberty; consummation of the marriage ( raunā ). 1. Maiṅ hāṅ joban-mātī, guiyāṅ, kab hoëgo gauno? Bārī sī umirīyā, chhatiyāṅ madh meṅ bharī haiṅ, Bin rasiyā raheo na jāë, na jānūṅ kab hoëgo gauno ? Farrukh. Wom. song. The woman in me strong I feel, my gauna would twere nigh! I'm young in years, my dear, my breasts with passion heave and sigh, Without my love I cannot stay, when will my gaunā be! 2. Saiyāṅ na lāë gavanvāṅ, hamār larkaiyāṅ bītī jāëṅ! E. Wom. song. My husband comes not yet to take me home, my youth is fleeting fast! 3. Phūlī phūlī gaune ko, ṭhasak nikal gaī raune ko . Prov. With bounce and swelling pride to her spouse she came a young bride, from her parents returning her first visit paid, all her pride in the dust is soon laid. 4. Sāthan merī! gaune kī kah …
s. m. Bringing home a wife.

r: gaunāʼolī, gaunā, wālī, olī, lī, rī

more: s. f. A hole in a path (such as those made by the feet of animals).

s.f. A young wife brought to her husband's home (see gaunā).
s. f. Power, opportunity.
s.f. Continued effort, perseverance, diligence; pertinacity.

Brij. n. 1. A villager. 2. A blockhead. 3. A father-in-law. 4. An abuse.
s.f. (Dakh.) = gaun, 'opportunity,' q.v. s.f. Opportunity, advantage, occasion; need

or gauṅ gāṅṭhnā (apnī), To attain (one's) end, serve (one's) purpose.
